questions.text: How would you describe the primary evidence that your target customers have an urgent need for your solution?

options.text: We have signed LOIs, paid pilots, or strong revenue traction
We have qualitative validation (interviews, surveys, waitlist)
We have strong logical/industry evidence but limited direct customer data
We're still building out our customer validation

questions.text: How do you currently size your market opportunity?

options.text: Bottom-up: built from customer segment data and verified assumptions
Top-down: based on industry reports and a percentage capture

questions.text: How clearly can you articulate why this is the right moment for your company — the specific market shift, technology change, or regulatory event that makes now the window?

ratingLabels.4: We have compelling research but need to lock it in

questions.text: Which of the following best describes your competitive moat?
How would you rate the clarity of your competitive positioning — your ability to explain why you win vs. each major alternative?
What is your current revenue stage?

options.text: Growing revenue (provide ARR figure in the notes field)

questions.notePlaceholder: If growing revenue, please provide your ARR figure here

questions.text: Do you know your customer acquisition cost (CAC) and lifetime value (LTV)?

options.text: Yes — both are calculated and based on real data
Partially — we have estimates but they're not fully validated
We've thought about it but haven't formalized the numbers

questions.text: How long did it take to close your last three deals (from first contact to signed contract)?
questions.helperText: If you're pre-revenue, please select the option that best applies to your pilot or early customer conversations.

questions.text: How would you describe your current team's coverage of the key functions you'll need to scale over the next 18 months?

options.text: Covered for now, with 1–2 known gaps we're actively addressing
We have meaningful gaps we haven't yet resolved
Our team is lean and several areas are under-resourced

questions.text: Do you currently have a board of directors or advisory board that provides meaningful challenge and strategic input — not just support?

options.text: Yes — active board with relevant experience and genuine oversight
We have advisors but not a formal board yet
We have a board but it's largely passive at this stage

questions.text: Are all founders and key executives under a formal vesting schedule?

options.text: Yes, all founders and key team members are on vesting

questions.text: How would you rate the quality of your current financial model?

options.text: Built out, but not yet stress-tested or investor-reviewed
Detailed with clear assumptions, scenarios, and unit economics

questions.text: For the capital you're looking to raise, do you have a clear deployment plan with specific milestones it will fund, and what those milestones unlock?

options.text: Yes — specific milestones with clear logic for the next raise
Mostly — we have a general plan but not fully mapped
We know the amount we need but haven't mapped it to milestones in detail

questions.text: Have you modeled what happens if growth is 30–40% slower than your base case?

options.text: Yes — we have downside scenarios with adjusted timelines and capital needs
Partially — we've thought about it but haven't modeled it formally

questions.text: If you had to describe your company in two minutes with no slides — to a smart investor who knows nothing about your space — how confident are you in that pitch right now?
